title: La Comédie humaine - Volume 11. Scènes de la vie parisienne - Tome 03
summaries: "La Comédie humaine - Volume 11. Scènes de la vie parisienne - Tome 03" by Balzac is part of a monumental literary project written between 1829 and 1850. This volume belongs to Balzac's ambitious effort to create a "natural history of society" through over ninety interconnected works. The collection systematically explores social groups and societal mechanisms through hundreds of recurring characters who reappear across different novels. Balzac captures the rise of capitalism, the power of money, and the complexity of human nature in nineteenth-century France. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
subjects: France -- Social life and customs -- 19th century -- Fiction
